title: rubia peregrina subsp. longifolia
intro: Rubia peregrina subsp. longifolia, also known as the 'Long-leafed Wild Rubia', is a subspecies of Rubia peregrina, a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides and other compounds. The health benefits of Rubia peregrina subsp. longifolia include the potential treatment of digestive issues, skin problems, and as a natural dye. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are typically made from the roots and leaves, and can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
